description: The measurement from base to top; the quality of being tall. 'Raeli' names vertical extent upward—how far something rises, how elevated something stands.
sound_symbolism: The word rises. 'Ra' reaches upward, ascent beginning. The vowel 'e' opens to elevation. Then 'li' lifts to completion, height achieved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'shiro raeli' (the tree's height). Related to adjective 'raelu' (tall). Opposite dimension from 'nusho' (depth).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Height offers perspective—elevation allows seeing further, understanding more of the whole.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Height meant safety and vision—high ground for defense, tall places for lookouts.
